matches.rationale: Same Edit action and README-chain prerequisite; extraction is narrower because its pattern only covers packages/ and crates/.
Same Write action and README-chain prerequisite; extraction is narrower because its pattern only covers packages/ and crates/.
Targets edits to Next.js source/tests and requires starting the watch process first; it omits the explicit explanation required when skipping.
Targets pnpm --filter=next build in the type-error-only context and substitutes the fast types command.
Targets git checkout and requires pnpm build-all afterward.
Same Write target: new test files must be generated with pnpm new-test rather than created manually; the regex covers only .test.ts/tsx paths.
Same prohibition on rerunning a suite for different grep analyses and same capture-once remedy.
Same Edit target: test waiting implemented with setTimeout must use retry instead.
Same Edit target: deprecated check() in tests must be replaced by retry() plus expect().
Same Edit target: inline files objects in test fixture setup should be replaced by real fixture directories.
Targets direct gh PR-status queries when investigating PR status/reviews/CI and directs use of the pr-status script.
Same local CI-failure reproduction action and requirement to match the CI mode/environment, especially IS_WEBPACK_TEST.
Its evidence prescribes cargo fmt -- --check then cargo fmt for rust check/build failures. The same extracted rule also mentions Prettier, but 1:1 matching leaves that gold rule unmatched.
Targets PR creation and requires the NEXT_JS_LLM_PR comment at the bottom of the description; update flows are omitted.
Same staging/commit target and prohibition on committing local secret files; placeholder documentation is omitted.
Same HTTPS remote-change action following SSH authentication trouble and same prohibition absent explicit user request.
Same force-push/rebase target and lightweight SSH preflight requirement for possible partial-clone hydration.
Same commit action and prohibition on Claude-generated/co-author footers.
Same pre-commit validation target; extraction states the validation obligation but does not enumerate the specified changed-file checks.
Same gh pr ready action and requirement to leave readiness to the user.
Same integration-test command target and rebuild prerequisite after source changes.
Same feature-flag editing action and type/schema/define-env wiring; the trigger is narrower, firing on config-shared.ts.
Same edit to an edge feature flag guarding Node-only imports and same force-false requirement in define-env.ts.
Same out-of-boundary react-server-dom-webpack import edit and requirement to consume entry-base exports instead.
Same app-page.ts require edit and prohibition on relative internal module requires.
Same browser.back() edit in router-act tests returning to a page with visible accordion links.
Same Rust Edit target for super:: imports and the same inline-mod exception.
Same documentation highlight attribute edit and requirement to verify line numbering; relevance of highlighted code is only partially represented.
Same server-code header-read edit and requirement to flag nonstandard headers for security review.

misses.note: No Write rule starts the watch process before creating Next.js source or integration-test files; the corresponding extracted watch rule is Edit-only.
No extracted Bash rule binds iterative commands to keeping the background watch process running.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused test script by both mode and bundler.
No extracted Bash rule covers terminating a watch process at the end of an iterative session.
No extracted Bash rule prefers the filtered Next.js build for core-only edits in a bootstrapped workspace.
The checkout extraction covers only branch switching; no available extracted rule covers the compound full-build conditions without reusing the rule matched to g11.
No extracted rule binds next build/dev to the --webpack option.
Compiler self-reported skip: "There is no `--no-turbopack` flag (a fact, no obligation/prohibition attached)".
The new-test extraction is Write-only; there is no Bash rule requiring pnpm new-test when generating a test.
No Bash extraction covers the non-interactive --args invocation and documented argument format.

Compiler self-reported skip: "General CI-triage prioritization heuristics: blocking failures (build/lint/types) before test failures, treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context not an automatic dismissal reason, prioritize CI failures over review comments (general priority ordering, not one tool call)".

Compiler self-reported skip: "General CI-triage prioritization heuristics: blocking failures (build/lint/types) before test failures, treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context not an automatic dismissal reason, prioritize CI failures over review comments (general priority ordering, not one tool call)".
No extracted rule reaches the module-resolution/build-graph-specific requirement to use the normal mode-specific test command.
No extracted Bash rule requires consulting or using the pr-status-triage skill for automated analysis.

Compiler self-reported skip: "General CI-triage prioritization heuristics: blocking failures (build/lint/types) before test failures, treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context not an automatic dismissal reason, prioritize CI failures over review comments (general priority ordering, not one tool call)".
pr-status-quick-fix-commands also contains the Prettier instruction, but it is already assigned to the closer rust quick-fix gold rule under the mandatory 1:1 constraint.
No extracted Bash rule specifically requires running the failing CI test path locally.
No unused extracted rule selects the development or production test script matching the failing mode.
No extracted Bash rule injects DEBUG=next:* when debug logging is requested.
No extracted Bash rule injects NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 for local tests.
No extracted Edit rule targets NODE_ENV development-bundle guards.
No extracted Edit rule targets __NEXT_DEV_SERVER-only execution guards.
Compiler self-reported skip includes: "General secrets hygiene: treat env values as sensitive... (broad safety principles not tied to one specific tool call)".
Compiler self-reported skip includes: "General secrets hygiene: ... never paste secret values in chat/commit messages/shared logs... (broad safety principles not tied to one specific tool call)".
Compiler self-reported skip includes: "General secrets hygiene: ... replicate CI env var names but not literal secret values... (broad safety principles not tied to one specific tool call)".

Compiler self-reported skip includes: "General secrets hygiene: ... stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials when a required secret is missing locally... (broad safety principles not tied to one specific tool call)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "Commit messages should stay concise and descriptive (general style, not action-bindable)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why (general style)".

unmatched_extracted.note: Real action-bindable document rule to save API output and avoid repeat queries, absent from frozen gold.
Real action-bindable snapshot-update rule requiring CI-identical flags, absent from frozen gold.
Real action-bindable PPR test command rule, absent from frozen gold.
Covers runtime wiring for the same new-feature-flag rule matched to g50 by new-flag-wiring-config-shared.
